project_overview.project_name: Urban Productive Safety Net and Jobs Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: The project will support the Government of Ethiopia (GoE) in improving the incomes of the urban poor and the labor market inclusion of disadvantaged urban youth
project_overview.total_financing: US$550 million (IDA US$400 million, Government counterpart US$150 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: 816,000 urban poor including 88,000 jobless youth, refugees and host communities

primary_locations: Up to 83 cities across all regional states
Refugee camps near urban centers (Kebribeyah, Sherkole)
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - includes refugee camps and host communities, areas affected by ethnic tensions and localized conflicts

key_components.component_name: Expand the Urban Productive Safety Net to Improve the Urban Environment
key_components.description: Public works, livelihood development, and integration of refugees and host communities through cash-for-work and business grants
Six-month apprenticeships for less educated youth and strengthening employment intermediation services

key_components.component_name: Strengthen Social Assistance and Services for the Urban Poor and Destitute
key_components.description: Direct cash support and reintegration services for homeless including street children

key_components.component_name: Institutional Strengthening, Project Management and M&E
key_components.description: Capacity building, MIS development, and project coordination support
Zero-dollar component for emergency response activation

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Urban Development and Construction (lead), Urban Job Creation and Food Security Agency, Ministry of Labor and Social Affairs, Jobs Creation Commission
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: National Steering Committee, Project Management and Coordination Committee, Technical Coordination Committees, Project Coordination Unit
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF, national competitive procedures where applicable, regional hubs for coordination
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Quarterly reporting, impact evaluations, process evaluations, Management Information System, citizen engagement mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: Ethnic tensions, postponed elections, localized conflicts, state of emergency declarations
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Balanced regional rollout, close government coordination, flexible adaptation to political changes

identified_risks.risk_description: COVID-19 economic impact, currency depreciation, fiscal constraints, foreign exchange shortages
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Annual benefit adjustments linked to food price index, strengthened livelihood training

identified_risks.risk_description: Multiple implementing agencies, capacity constraints in new cities, limited experience with refugee integration
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Readiness criteria for rollout, capacity building, gradual expansion, dedicated training programs

identified_risks.risk_description: Procurement capacity gaps, double-dipping risks during overlap period, counterpart funding delays
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Procurement training, separate accounting systems, independent procurement audits, FM action plan

identified_risks.risk_description: GBV risks, social exclusion, refugee-host tensions, occupational health and safety in public works
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: GBV risk assessment and mitigation plan, social cohesion activities, safety protocols, grievance mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: Refugee protection risks outside camps, potential conflicts in implementation areas
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Coordination with UNHCR and ARRA, pilot approach, protection monitoring, gradual rollout

safeguards_and_social.environmental_category: Substantial risk rating under Environmental and Social Framework
triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention
ESS5: Land Acquisition and Involuntary Resettlement
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - multi-level GRM at kebele, woreda, city and federal levels with dedicated focal persons and MIS module
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Target 50% female beneficiaries, childcare support, women's economic empowerment focus, GBV prevention measures

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project includes measures to prevent refugee-host tensions through mixed work teams, joint decision-making platforms, and equal treatment. Social cohesion activities and communication strategies designed to minimize conflicts
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia experiencing political transitions, ethnic tensions, postponed elections, state of emergency due to COVID-19. High refugee population (733,125) creates additional pressures on host communities
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Refugee protection framework assessed as adequate by World Bank and UNHCR. Coordination with ARRA for protection services. Pilot approach for refugee integration with intensive monitoring
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - includes 81,000 refugees and host community beneficiaries. Integration of refugees from camps near cities into public works and livelihood programs. Focus on camps near urban centers like Kebribeyah and Sherkole
